TORNADO DESTROYED AN ATTACHED GARAGE ON ONE HOME AND DESTROYED A COUPLE OF OUT BUILDINGS TO GO ALONG WITH MAINLY TREE DAMAGE. THE TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN 6 WSW OF FOREST LAKE IN RURAL ANOKA COUNTY AND LIFTED 4 NE OF HUGO IN RURAL WASHINGTON COUNTY. EF1 WITH A MAXIMUM WIND SPEED OF 90 MPH, DAMAGE LENGTH 8.8 MILES, AND A MAXIMUM WIDTH OF 100 YARDS.

TORNADO DEVELOPED ALONG A SEVERE LINE OF STORMS AND CAUSED AN ATTACHED GARAGE TO COLLAPSE, DESTROYED A COUPLE OUTBUILDINGS, AND PRODUCED A CONCENTRATED AREA OF HEAVIER TREE DAMAGE. MAX WIND 90 MPH, PATH LENGTH 8.8 MILES. IT ENDED 4 MILES NORTHEAST OF HUGO. THE DURATION OF THE TORNADO EVENT WAS 9 MINUTES.
